prompt: An audit export consumer is producing two kinds of damage after a proxy rollout. Some non-ASCII notes arrive with replacement characters only when proxy chunk sizes are small, while exports interleaving several account channels omit one channel's first fragment and occasionally most of that channel. Raw byte checksums agree at both ends and replaying the same bytes as one large chunk looks healthy. Single-channel ASCII exports, same-channel retransmit suppression, and malformed-sequence rejection all remain healthy. The wire contract is the existing record-separator JSON stream, sequence numbers restart independently for each channel, and chunks are arbitrary byte slices. Preserve streaming and bounded buffering, do not change the wire format or buffer the whole export, and limit edits to the candidate files.
